content.problemStatement:
You are given a set SSS of KKK numbers which is a set of first KKK powers of 222 (ie. 111,222,444,888,...)

Given NNN you have to construct an Array AAA[000, ..., N−1N-1N−1]  of length NNN such that A[i]A[i]A[i] must belong to SSS for all iii, 000 <= iii < NNN.

Find the maximum value of A[0]A[0]A[0] XOR A[1]A[1]A[1] XOR ... XOR A[N−1]A[N-1]A[N−1] that you can achieve. Here XOR is bitwise xor operator. Also, tell the number of arrays AAA that gives this maximum XOR.

Print both the numbers mod 109+710^9+7109+7.


Input Format

First line of Input contains the no of test cases T.
Each of the next T lines contains two integers: N and K.


Output Format

For every test case, print two space-separated integers: maximum possible XOR and the no of possible ways respectively, modulo 109+710^9+7109+7.
constraints: Sum of K over all testcases <= 1000000
content.inputFormat: The first line contains the number of test cases T. Each of the next T lines contains two integers: N and K.
content.outputFormat: For every test case, print two space-separated integers: maximum possible XOR and the number of possible ways respectively, modulo 10^9+7.

sampleTestCases.explanation: The set S is {1, 2, 4}. The maximum XOR is 7, which can be achieved by the combination {1, 2, 4}. There are 6 ways to arrange these elements, so the output is 7 6.
The set S is {1, 2, 4, 8}. The maximum XOR is 15, which can be achieved by the combination {1, 2, 4, 8}. There are 24 ways to arrange these elements, so the output is 15 24.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we need to find the maximum possible XOR of the elements in the array A and the number of ways to achieve this XOR. We can start by generating the set S of the first K powers of 2. Then, we can iterate over all possible combinations of elements from S to find the maximum XOR. We also need to keep track of the number of ways to achieve this XOR. We can use modular arithmetic to avoid overflow and ensure that the results are within the range of 10^9+7.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * K * N) — the time complexity is dominated by the iteration over all possible combinations of elements from S.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(K) — the space complexity is dominated by the storage of the set S.
